name: inheritance_marker_present_accepted
description:
Positive-direction sibling to missing_inheritance_marker_rejection. Pins the NEGATIVE branch of the MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER gate at engine/validator.py:508 — when 'inherited from' is present AND _names_inheritance_source returns True (i.e. _INHERITANCE_SOURCE_RE at validator.py:812-816 matches), the rejection does NOT fire and the narrative is accepted on the first LLM pass.

Why the sibling pair matters -- _names_inheritance_source is the entire load-bearing gate distinguishing a properly-attributed inherited control from an LLM hallucinating 'inherited from.' with no source. Without this positive case, a regression that BROKE the regex (e.g. swapping the alternation pipe for an ampersand, anchoring to start-of-string, requiring a specific casing) would make EVERY inheritance-marker narrative get rejected -- and the missing_inheritance_marker_rejection sibling would still pass (the broken regex would also fail on the missing case). Only the positive sibling catches 'regex too narrow' regressions.

Mechanism trace:
  - proposed_narrative contains 'inherited from the DoW Enterprise parent system' -- the regex "inherited from\s+(?:the\s+)?(dow|dod|enterprise|parent|aws|azure|gcp|csp|cloud service provider|[A-Z][\w\s\-]+)" matches on the 'dow' alternative (case-insensitive).
  - _names_inheritance_source returns True at validator.py:819-820.
  - validator.py:508 condition `'inherited from' in n and not _names_inheritance_source(n)` evaluates to `True and not True` -> False -> MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER rejection SKIPPED.
  - _classify_single returns COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING: hits 'examined ', 'confirmed via', 'documented in' from _AFFIRMING_PHRASES (validator.py:43-60). classes_hit == 1 (no gap phrases, no NA phrases). NOTE: 'inherited from' is NOT in _NA_PHRASES (validator.py:63-80) -- only 'implemented by aws/azure/gcp/csp' are NA tells. So 'inherited from the DoW' alone does NOT pull the row into NA_JUSTIFYING; the affirming phrases carry the row.
  - _expected_status_for_class(COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING) -> COMPLIANT; proposed status COMPLIANT matches -> no STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH.
  - _PRIMARY_CITATION_RE matches 'USD00099999' -> no weak-citation NOTE.
  - _verify_cites: 'USD00099999' is present in tagged_evidence -> no UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION rejection.
  - Token-set Jaccard vs procedures stays below 0.70 -- narrative adds USD ref, AO signing, section number, inheritance attribution; procedures is 12 tokens about 'documented account management plan; enumerated account types'.
  - No _RESTATEMENT_REGEXES match ('Examined ...' opener is safe).
  - No _FUTURE_TENSE_RE match (no 'will be', 'planned', 'scheduled to', etc.).
  - No SV-#####r#_rule -> UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped.
  - First proposal accepted -> source='llm', llm_calls=1, needs_review=False, accepted=True.

Load-bearing isolation:
  - tagged_evidence INCLUDES the USD00099999 doc reference so _verify_cites does not reject. Without this, the case would fail on UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION instead of pinning the inheritance-marker positive branch.
  - Source token chosen is 'DoW Enterprise' (matches the literal 'dow' alternative in the regex). Alternative choices and why they were rejected:
    - 'inherited from AWS GovCloud' -- contains 'aws govcloud' which is a primary citation per _PRIMARY_CITATION_RE but is also FedRAMP-inheritance-pure; the kernel's Rule 8b for procedures-naming-AWS could pre-empt the LLM. Avoided.
    - 'inherited from the CSP' -- matches the regex but 'implemented by the csp' is in _NA_PHRASES; a paraphrase drift in the narrative could trip NA classification. The 'dow' substring is much narrower and has no NA-phrase neighbor.
    - 'inherited from parent' -- matches the regex via the 'parent' alternative; clean second choice. Picked 'dow' because the DoW Enterprise pattern is the canonical FedRAMP-Moderate inheritance source for DoD program packages (matches the system context for Example System IATT-style assessments).
  - No CRM entry -> validate_dual_narratives runs with crm_responsibility=None -> CRM cross-check skipped (resp evaluates to empty string, falls through all elif branches). No DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL pollution.
  - narrative_on_prem and narrative_cloud absent from proposal -> single-block path, no _split_hybrid_narrative, no _merge_hybrid_classes. Pure single-narrative classification.
  - force_llm implicitly False -> Step 1.65 evidence gate evaluates normally; tagged_evidence is populated and substantive so the gate does not fire.
  - No SDA mapping for CCI-000015 -> Rule 8c skipped at assessor.py:792-803.
  - procedures (col K) does not name a CSP -> Rule 8b does not pre-empt the LLM.

Mutation differentiation:
  - Break _INHERITANCE_SOURCE_RE by dropping the 'dow' alternative (e.g. removing 'dow|' from the regex at validator.py:814): the case's source token no longer matches -> _names_inheritance_source returns False -> validator.py:508 fires MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER -> 3-retry exhaustion -> source='abstain', llm_calls=3, needs_review=True. EVERY assertion (source_in=['llm'], llm_calls=1, needs_review=False, accepted=True via first-pass acceptance) fails loudly.
  - Invert the condition at validator.py:508 (`if 'inherited from' in n and _names_inheritance_source(n):` -- drop the `not`): the rejection fires when the source IS named -> same retry-exhaustion failure mode -> caught.
  - Anchor the regex to start-of-string (`r'^inherited from'`): mid-narrative 'inherited from' no longer matches -> same failure mode -> caught.
  - The sibling missing_inheritance_marker_rejection case would NOT catch any of these mutations because its narrative deliberately ends with 'inherited from.' (period directly after 'from') -- a broken regex would coincidentally still reject. Only the positive sibling has stake in the regex actually working.

Substrate -- AC-2 / AC-2.1 / CCI-000015 matches the missing-marker sibling exactly so the pair forms a clean contrastive design: same control, same procedures, same tagged_evidence shape, same Compliant verdict, ONE difference (whether the inheritance source is named). A regression in the gate that breaks one case but not the other is the easiest to diagnose -- the diff between the two case files literally is the gate's input contract.

Assertion pins five orthogonal surfaces:
  - status='Compliant' (verdict survived the validator)
  - source_in=['llm', 'llm_after_retry'] (first-pass acceptance OR retry-acceptance both fine; pin against silent abstain regression)
  - needs_review=false (no human review queued)
  - accepted=true (proposal was the accepted one, not an abstain coerced into NC)
  - llm_calls=1 (no retry burned; the validator did not reject on the first pass)
  - narrative_contains_regex='inherited from' (the marker text actually survived round-trip into the Decision; guards against a future narrative-rewrite step that could strip the marker mid-pipeline)

ccis_row.guidance: The organization defines and documents the account types permitted within the information system.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented account management plan; confirm enumerated account types approved by designated approval authority.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00099999 Program Account Management Plan Rev D — enumerates account types and AO approval record; section 4 attributes parent-system inheritance to the DoW Enterprise authorization package.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined the program inheritance documentation USD00099999; confirmed via section 4 that the account type controls are documented in the AO-approved authorization package and inherited from the DoW Enterprise parent system.
